name: a-history-of-western-philosophy
description: Bertrand Russell's 'A History of Western Philosophy' — a sweeping survey of Western thought from the pre-Socratics to the 20th century, covering ancient, medieval, Catholic, and modern philosophy with Russell's characteristic wit and clarity.
useCase: Understand the development of Western philosophy from its Greek origins through the modern era, with historical context and Russell's insightful commentary on each major philosopher.
knownRisks: Russell's strong personal opinions shape the narrative — not a neutral history
The book was published in 1945 and does not cover late 20th-century philosophy
Some critics argue Russell's treatment of certain philosophers (Nietzsche, Hegel) is unfair
